item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is one of those scps that i was amazed i hadn't upvoted yet, love the connections to 2000, one of my favourite scips and i instantly got all of the references. needless to say, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

Just some quick notes.

  1. I personally think hume theory is bunk. Anomalies are anomalous b/c there is absolutely no explanation for it. However, I don't mind crosslink or reference to it, because I have it as headcanon that in-universe, hume theory is garbage, and is simply a lie propagated by O5.
  2. I like your Resu reference.
  3. I like that the Foundation is using type greens help contain. I can't suspend enough belief to think that SCP Foundation, without using anomalies themselves, can contain some of the things they encounter. You'd have to believe that average joe could keep Superman in a box.

Call it x-men or superheroes or cringeworthy or anything at all. There's a place for it if the execution is right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is quite fun itself. An unstoppable generator of Greek "gods", and the only known way to stop hostile psuedo-gods is to use others empowered by this anomoly? Cool idea, a bit unusual for the Foundation but desperate times call for desperate measures.

However. I'm not able to give it a +1 since it's impossible to take seriously due to the articles it links to. The Hume article that reads like a salespitch is not great, but this article just loses all it's impact by linking to an explanation of a threat level system where an example given is "causes the destruction of the known universe if it gets headbutted by a one-legged saxophonist clad in a pink tutu made of 100% cotton". There's no recovering a skip's gravitas after something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this SCP. Take your 200th upvote +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
um, SCP-2000 is under Yellowstone. And…

Spoilers
Told Ya

And as the author stated earlier in this forum, SCP-3480's pooping out reality warpers because it's a portal to an alternate future where SCP-2000 is broken, an XK happened and the broken remains of SCP-2000 is constant creating clones of SCPF-researchers/D-class, who also are type greens due to the broken SRAs. They're all basically living in hell in alt-future Yellowstone, but some of them fall through the portal, where they either co-operate with the SCPF if they were researchers or lashing out if they were D-class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 brother

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realized I forgot to make a crosslink to Fieldstone’s amazing tale about this skip, On the Slope of Olympus. That has now been fixed :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed reading this one, especially the connection to SCP-2000, although I can't seem to figure out the connection between yellowstone and Mt. Olympus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you make a good point here, and I've updated it accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only real gripe with this is that Level 5 clearance is generally only given to the O5 council, the Ethics Committee, and really important Researchers and soldiers (in my headcanon, only around 30 people have full L5 clearance at a time). Level 4 clearance is also given to really influential people only, like Site Directors and the such. Having only these 30 people (which obviously have other responsibilities and duties) managing this task force is a bit excessive, IMO.

Then again, it's just my headcanon and this is a great skip anyways. +1

item.title: +100! :D
item.content:encoded: Thanks to everyone who enjoyed! This was my first skip and it's my first piece to break 100, so this means a lot to me. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem at all, I appreciate the insight. You have a point about the dangers, though that is mitigated by the paratech kill switches and the fact that these people are (clones of) Foundation personnel. I actually have a tale series planned around Omega-12 that explores a lot of this, and many characters in that tale series will actually feel similar to you: that the task force is too risky and its members should be liquidated.

I wrote this partially as a tribute to 2000 and partially as a way to answer the question raised in early site works on reality benders: what do you do when you're up against that 1% of greens that can't just be killed by quick wits and a bullet? Kaktus gave an answer to that via a reality bending superweapon in SCP-001- The Children, Fieldstone just last month gave an answer to it in the form of another superweapon called project orpheus in his tale Parts, and I gave an answer here in the form of Omega-12. All of these incorporate reality benders in their functioning (though its a bit more complicated in Kaktus' case) but mine is the only one where the greens are alive and willing. In some ways, that's actually less risky than the others.

Re: the Thaumiel bit, the other thaumiel humanoids aren't dangerous but we do have potentially dangerous or intelligent thaumiels that are not human. Ie 1968, 3095, and arguably 3000, among others. Also this might be a headcanon thing, but I think that anomalous assets would be internally classified as Thaumiel even if they don't have an scp number. So samsara would be a thaumiel asset, every member of Alpha-9 would be thaumiel, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think so. Samsara bothers me for much the same reason as this does, although they at least would be easier for the foundation to neutralize if they went rogue (pulling the plug on their incubators and shooting them a bunch should do it, IIRC). Not so for reality benders.

As for Resurrection… that whole series is about the Foundation figuring out if anomalous task forces work. Somebody'll have to resolve that series before I start being more open to accepting similar things into my headcannon.

I think part of the problem for me is actually specific to it being an MTF. The Foundation has shown itself fairly willing to work with a sympathetic part of an anomalous phenomenon in order to contain a more troubling part. But for the most part those examples are contained to individual scips. Other times individuals are co-opted onto task forces because of some bit of expertise (Agent Navarro, SCP-085). But to organize a task force made up entirely of the most dangerous kind of anomaly, beings who can reshape reality on a whim… I mean, what the hell is the contingency in case they decide the Foundation needs to be more like the one in the reality they left behind?

A quick perusal shows no other humanoid Thaumiel anomalies aside from SCP-179 (who doesn't physically affect anything on earth or endanger the veil) and SCP-3667 (who are D-class and therefore don't have enough freedom to cause trouble).

If there were one or two benders that assisted an otherwise-normal MTF, and were confined to containing this anomaly, maybe I'd be more willing to buy it.

Oh, and sorry if I'm beating a dead horse. Since you took the trouble to respond I wanted to try and better explain my objections.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel ya, this is certainly not everyone's cup of tea. Though to be fair this is what, the third anomalous task force we've got?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted because the surface-level callback to scp-2000 and greek mythology don't feel worth giving the Foundation a team of x-men.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers
- Hide

Basically the -2 instances are coming from an alternate future in which humanity was destroyed by an XK and SCP-2000 was heavily damaged. It's malfunctioning paratech is what's causing all this mess, and the "gods" are imperfect clones of researchers and D class who have become reality benders thanks to the malfunctioning SRAs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…

To summarize, Mt. Olympus produces gods? And the Foundation is somehow responsible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe I didn't notice that in the 5 months this has been up haha.

Hell, on my SCP, I got "occulting" and "occluding" mixed up.

Apparently, nobody noticed that mixup for two years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed! And derp, error fixed. Can't believe I didn't notice that in the 5 months this has been up haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1.

I'm always a sucker for anomalies being used to contain anomalies - desperate measures, unlikely alliances, that sort of thing. And in this case it actually makes sense because they're Foundation agents. Also, good foreshadowing - you had me going from 'One site with mass SRAs? Is this a throwaway reference to 2000?' to 'Wait, this is 2000!'

One last thing.

 It is thus theorized that SCP-3480 acts as hole through which local reality is drawn.

I think you're missing an 'a' here, unless I'm the one missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added an Omega-12 logo made by the amazing SunnyClockwork. Thanks Sunny!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact that the Foundation REALLY does not like doing this.

item.title: Re: minor stuff
item.content:encoded: Actually, I think if you're looking to express an exceedingly small number, you'll want to use scientific notation: 3.49 x 10-16 or something, with appropriate black-boxes. Just having the 10 with the negative exponent is odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made a major edit to trim the fat and improve the narrative flow of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bingo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohhhh, I see.

The reason why some the reality benders are malicious is because they are D-Class, which, in my eyes, hate the foundation or fear it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. Maybe put all the stuff about Omega-12 after the note in the same dropdown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slow. Clap.

+1

If I had to change anything, I'd do away with the unaltered document. It doesn't add a ton, except serving as a shovel through which to dig out the last of the lede. A little ambiguity there would have been better than laying bare.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to like this. I really really did.

Unfortunately, it hit a little too "on the nose" for me, especially regarding the idea that this is where greek mythology comes from.

I'm not a very big fan of SCPs which "explain" parts of history, as they tend to be kind of flat, and one dimensional.

The final interview would have had some great impact, if it had been the crux of the article, but it felt like an afterthought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you ended the article on that interview, it might have some impact when one realises that it is SCP-2000. Maybe how that ended up in Mount Olympus might not be immediately clear.

The Level 5 document does not really add much, since the interview handled the reveal better. The Greek myth reference can hold more water if the instances generated have stronger parallels to Greek mythology. Currently, they seem to be humans of varying unusual traits that can bend reality.

Perhaps the main point in the Level 5 version is to state that they are using reality benders to fight the instances off. I understand you have thematic reasons for it, but the development of the story here still did not convince me that reality benders are needed.

item.title: Re: minor stuff
item.content:encoded: Fixed! Thanks for the help. Hope you enjoyed it.

item.title: minor stuff
item.content:encoded:
Upon commencement of White event, Area 13 XACTS is to be used to temporally isolate Mt. ███████ from surrounding reality in order to reduce likelihood of CK class Reality Restructuring Scenario.

censoring Olympus here is a bit nonsensical since it's already mentioned several times in both the Containment Procedure and the article itself

aprroximately 1-██ Humes on the Caldmann-Rzewski Hume scale.

spelling error on "approximately", and no mater how many times you divided or multiplied it with itself, one is always one, so you might be looking for 10-██ instead

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Hope you all enjoy! Big thanks to Tretter and MrWrong for critque and advice, and also thanks to FortuneFavorsBold for writing SCP-2000, which wound up becoming an important MacGuffin for this piece. Caldmann and Rzewski also deserve credit for their excellent Hume articles. 3480 (as well as the 480 hours during the Class Five's White event) was chosen in reference to the battle of Thermopylae, which took place in 480 BC.

+ Show Image Sources
- Hide

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mytikas_at_sunset_(Olympus_mt.).jpg

Image from wikimedia commons used under Creative Commons 4.0

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Amazing_halo_effect_(1336635365).jpg

Image from wikimedia commons used under Creative Commons 2.0

If you enjoyed, check out my other stuff here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/modern-erasmus-author-page

edit: New header image was created by Kath
